Try HARD to REFUTE this proposed change to the POLARIS pipeline. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel if needed.

CHANGE: Body-level INFERENCE tier in the production verifier (C2)
FILE/SEAM: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:2596-2621 (NEUTRAL branch; verdict at 2930), reusing _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE from analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226 and _span_grounds_sentence:138-152 :: The gate's boundary is exactly: conjunction of two sources admitted, inference from two sources killed. The judge already sees both spans concatenated (combined_span at :2576) and returns NEUTRAL because the prompt's NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) defines interpretation as failure. The kill is one enum away from a keep.
WHAT: New env PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER (default OFF). In the NEUTRAL branch at :2596-2621, BEFORE appending entailment_failed, check eligibility: (a) >=2 distinct ev_ids cited, (b) sentence matches _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE (hedge required), (c) all mechanical legs already passed (they run first, unchanged — every number/percent/qualifier is span-verified), (d) no named entity absent from the union of cited rows' full text (reuse the author-attribution + _span_grounds_sentence floor). If eligible, make ONE second judge call with a new PREMISE prompt: 'INFERENCE_OK iff every factual assertion in SENTENCE is present in SPAN and the ONLY content not in SPAN is a hedged interpretive relation over those assertions; otherwise NEUTRAL.' INFERENCE_OK => sentence kept with soft_warning='inference_tier' (audit-countable); anything else => existing drop. CONTRADICTED anywhere stays fatal; judge transport error stays fail-closed (:2919-2923 untouched).
CLAIMS: +0.5 to +1.0 overall, partially overlapping C1 (C1 is a synthesis section; C2 puts interpretation inline where the serial-summary problem lives). Counted at the overlap-discounted end. on insight (0.32), secondarily comprehensiveness (cross-source contrast survives in the body, not just an appendix section) (w=0.7)
FAILURE MODE THEY ADMIT: (a) The second judge call is the same model family that returns NEUTRAL — it may refuse INFERENCE_OK systematically, making the tier a no-op (detectable in telemetry, not harmful). (b) A lenient judge admits over-reaching mechanism claims dressed in 'may' — mitigated by the deterministic entity/number floors and by CONTRADICTED remaining fatal. (c) kept_fraction rises, weakening the tighter-retry pressure that keeps the writer conservative.
NEW FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: TWO-TIER ADMISSION CONTRACT. TIER F (FACT) — unchanged, byte-identical when all new flags are off: a sentence is admitted iff it clears every existing leg of verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098-2959) and `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (:2930). TIER I (INFERENCE, body, PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER): a sentence reaching the NEUTRAL branch (:2596-2621) is admitted iff ALL of: (1) it cites >=2 distinct evidence rows via provenance tokens — untokened sentences still die at :2186-2193, unchanged; (2) every MECHANICAL leg already passed — these run before entailment and are untouched, so every number, percent, integer, and binding qualifier in the sentence is already verified against the cited spans; (3) the first judge verdict was NEUTRAL — CONTRADICTED remains unconditionally fatal (:2607-2615 posture preserved); (4) the sentence carries an interpretive hedge cue (_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE, analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226) — a bare categorical assertion is ineligible; (5) no named entity (author, study, organization, venue) appears in the sentence that is absent from the union of the cited rows' full text (reusing _span_grounds_sentence:138-152 and the author-attribution floor at :264-302); (6) a SECOND judge call with a premise-decomposition prompt returns INFERENCE_OK, defined as: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is present in the SPAN, and the only content not in the SPAN is the hedged interpretive relation over those assertions — any other verdict, and any transport error, FAILS CLOSED to the existing entailment_failed drop (:2919-2923 posture preserved). Every Tier-I keep is tagged soft_warning='inference_tier' in the audit log and counted in telemetry, so the audit can enumerate exactly which sentences carry interpretation. TIER I-S (analyst section, existing dark-lane contract, unchanged from its authors' design): cited synthesis passes the deterministic anti-fabrication floor (every number in the union of cited sources' full text, >=2 content-word overlap); uncited interpretation passes _is_disclosable_interpretation (no decimals, no percent, no quantitative claim, no attribution absent from the pool, hedge cue mandatory); fabricated attributions and unhedged categoricals DROP; the section ships under an explicit interpretive disclosure. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, everywhere, in every tier: any sentence contradicted by its spans; any unhedged interpretive assertion; any sentence introducing a number, percent, or named entity not groundable in the union of its cited sources; any untokened body sentence; anything on judge transport error. WHY NO HALLUCINATION HOLE OPENS: the only novel content class admitted anywhere is a HEDGED INTERPRETIVE RELATION whose operands are each independently span-verified — numeric and entity fabrication remain blocked by deterministic legs that run BEFORE and independently OF the judge; the change converts exactly one verdict path (NEUTRAL + hedged + premise-verified) from drop to labeled-keep, and every such keep is individually enumerable in the audit log for post-hoc inspection.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
HALLUCINATION HOLE: the two-tier contract exempts inference sentences from span-grounding. Show the concrete path by which an UNGROUNDED or FALSE claim now reaches the report. If faithfulness can regress, this design is unshippable — POLARIS's whole moat is that it does not hallucinate. Be specific.

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". - verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. def verify_sentence_provenance(
    sentence: str,
    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
    *,
    require_number_match: bool = True,
    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
    allow_local_window_fallback: bool = True,
) -> SentenceVerification:
    """Verify every provenance token in a sentence.

    Checks:
      1. Evidence ID exists in pool.
      2. Span bounds are within evidence direct_quote length.
      3. If require_number_match AND the sentence contains numbers,
         each number must appear in the claimed span text.

    Phase 7 (#991) — Regime C router: when ``quantified_models`` is supplied
    (run-scoped registry keyed by (model_id, spec_hash)) AND the sentence carries
    a ``[#calc:...]`` token, the sentence is FORCE-ROUTED to
    ``verify_modeled_atom`` BEFORE the Regime-A machinery below and that result is
    returned. ``quantified_models=None`` (default) skips the router entirely →
    Regime A is byte-identical.

    I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) — ``allow_local_window_fallback`` (default
    True, byte-identical to pre-iter-2). When FALSE, the two gap-#18 LOCAL-WINDOW
    rescue sites are DISABLED so the sentence passes ONLY if its OWN bound span
    directly clears the content/numeric floor AND directly entails — no different
    in-row window may rescue it. This is set FALSE from ``_try_reanchor`` so the
    re-anchored token's FINAL BOUND SPAN ITSELF DIRECTLY SUPPORTS the claim; the
    Codex iter-1 P1 leak (a candidate span kept on the back of a DIFFERENT
    entailing window while the token stays bound to the non-entailing span) is
    structurally closed. Production strict_verify keeps the default True, so the
    Phase 0b grounded-prose rescue is unchanged outside the re-anchor accept gate.
    """
    if quantified_models is not None:
        _calc_matches = list(_CALC_TOKEN_RE.finditer(sentence))
        if _calc_matches:
            # Fail-closed sentence-shape rules (brief §1.5: AT MOST one calc number
            # per sentence). >1 calc token would leave the 2nd..Nth number
            # unverified after the 1st is checked; a MIXED [#calc:]+[#ev:] sentence
            # would launder an unverified Regime-A numeric claim through the calc
            # path. Both drop the whole sentence.
            if len(_calc_matches) > 1:
                return SentenceVerification(
                    sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
                    failure_reasons=["calc_multiple_tokens_in_sentence"],
                )
            if _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.search(sentence):
                return SentenceVerification(
                    sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
                    failure_reasons=["calc_mixed_with_ev_token"],
                )
            return verify_modeled_atom(
                sentence, _calc_matches[0], quantified_models, evidence_pool,
            )

    tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
    failures: list[str] = []
    # Phase 0b Delta 3 (I-meta-005, gap-#18): tracks whether the entailment
    # judge failed OPEN ((ENTAILED,"judge_error: ...")). Set in either judge
    # call below; consumed by the ON-mode fail-closed gate near the return.
    # HOISTED to function-body top (build agent EDIT-8 scope check): the
    # entailment block is nested inside `if require_number_match and
    # valid_token_found:`, so an init at the `if not failures:` site would NOT
    # be in scope at the return when valid_token_found is False (e.g. all
    # tokens fail evidence_not_in_pool) -> NameError. Init here so it is always
    # defined at the return.
    judge_error_flag = False

    # I-perm-004 (#1198) slice 3: when the gap-#18 local-window rescue ACCEPTS a sentence whose
    # narrow bound span did not directly entail, the [#ev] token is RE-POINTED to the rescue window
    # (the genuinely-entailing span) instead of shipping the original mis-pointed span. Captured here
    # at function scope as (evidence_id, start, end); applied at the return. None => no re-point.
    # Gated by PG_SPAN_RESOLVER (default OFF -> byte-identical) and SINGLE-token scope only (the
    # which-token-to-move combinatorics for multi-token sentences are out of scope, matching
    # _try_reanchor v1).
    reanchor_local_to: Optional[tuple[str, int, int]] = None

    # I-gen-005 Step 3b commit 1: verifier_text strips ALL citation
    # artifacts (provenance tokens + atom_NNN + bare [ev_XXX]) for ALL
    # internal verifier checks. The original sentence is preserved
    # on the returned SentenceVerification.sentence so downstream
    # (atom_refusal_validator, citation resolver) see atom_NNN tokens.
    # Per Codex Step 3b iter-2 P1: leaving atom_NNN in numeric or
    # content-overlap or entailment inputs would falsely drop valid
    # atom-cited sentences.
    sentence_for_numbers = _verifier_cleaned_text(sentence)

    if not tokens:
        # Sentences without provenance are rejected outright.
        return SentenceVerification(
            sentence=sentence,
            tokens=[],
            is_verified=False,
            failure_reasons=["no_provenance_token"],
        )

    # Fix-3b: When a sentence has multiple citations (e.g.,
    # [#ev:a][#ev:b][#ev:c]), the decimals in the sentence may come
    # from DIFFERENT cited spans — the ICER trial's 86.6% treatment
    # arm AND the placebo-comparator 47.6% from a different span. The
    # verifier should require that each decimal in the sentence is
    # found in SOME cited span (union), not in every span.

                failures.append(
                    f"trial_name_mismatch:{ev_ids}:"

        if not failures:

            )

                # "unsupported specificity of the authors' names" when the fetched span omits
                # the byline. Author-name binding ONLY (never a claim/number/topic); the
                # mechanical numeric/content gates above already ran on the span alone.
                combined_span += _author_metadata_annotation(
                    sentence_clean, tokens, evidence_pool,
                )
                verdict, reason = _get_judge().judge(
                    sentence_clean, combined_span,
                )
                _record_judge_outcome(verdict, reason)
                # Phase 0b Delta 3: the judge fails OPEN (entailment_judge.py:147)
                # returning ("ENTAILED","judge_error: ..."). Flag it; ON mode
                # fails-closed near the return. OFF leaves is_verified unchanged
                # (pre-0b fail-open preserved — filed as a separate gated issue).
                if verdict == "ENTAILED" and reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
                    judge_error_flag = True
                if verdict in ("NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED") and (
                    verdict == "CONTRADICTED" or not allow_local_window_fallback
                ):
                    # I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) P1: the re-anchor accept gate
                    # passes allow_local_window_fallback=False so the BOUND SPAN
                    # ITSELF must directly entail. A NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED on the
                    # narrow bound span fails closed HERE (under enforce) — no
                    # different in-row window may rescue a non-supporting candidate
                    # span. Mirrors the existing no-window branch (warn = log-only,
                    # off = unchanged); the rescue search below is skipped entirely.
                    #
                    # I-deepfix-001 U29 (span-imprecision leniency): a CONTRADICTED
                    # narrow cited span now ALWAYS fails closed here — regardless of
                    # allow_local_window_fallback — so a WIDER local window that
                    # ENTAILS can never rescue (mask) a narrow-span CONTRADICTION.
                    # The cited span actively REFUTES the claim; a different in-row
                    # window entailing is exactly the clinical-frame risk that must
                    # fail. Only NEUTRAL (imprecise/incomplete, NOT refuting) remains
                    # eligible for the bounded-window rescue below. This TIGHTENS the
                    # gate (removes a rescue path); it never relaxes one.
                    if mode == "enforce":
                        ev_ids = ",".join(sorted({t.evidence_id for t in tokens}))
                        failures.append(
                            f"entailment_failed:{ev_ids}:"
                            f"verdict={verdict}:reason={reason[:80]}"
                        )
                elif verdict == "NEUTRAL":

                    # passing whole `direct_quote` to the judge is the
                    # same architectural shape as the rejected
                    # whole-document numeric fallback — a 25k-char
                    # review can entail a claim from an unrelated
                    # paragraph. Fix: recover a BOUNDED local support
                    # window (same helper that catches numeric drops)
                    # then judge ONLY against that window.
                    #
                    # The local window is built from the sentence's
                    # decimals + content words (or content words alone
                    # for non-numeric claims). If no local window
                    # exists in any cited evidence, fail closed.
                    sentence_stripped_local = _strip_dose_patterns(sentence_clean)
                    sentence_stripped_local = _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE.sub(
                        " ", sentence_stripped_local,
                    )
                    sentence_stripped_local = _THRESHOLD_RE.sub(
                        " ", sentence_stripped_local,
                    )
                    sentence_dec_local = _decimals_in(sentence_stripped_local)
                    sentence_content_local = _content_words(sentence_stripped_local)

                    local_window_text: Optional[str] = None
                    local_ev_id: Optional[str] = None
                    # I-perm-004 (#1198) slice 3: keep the rescue window's OFFSETS (not just its

                    # accept, instead of shipping the claim bound to its original mis-pointed span
                    # (the idx-9 "shipped on a badge span" bug).
                    local_win: Optional[tuple[int, int]] = None
                    for tok in tokens:
                        ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
                        if ev is None:
                            continue
                        direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
                        # Use the same local-window finder; if the
                        # sentence has no decimals, fall back to a

# found in SOME cited span (union), not in every span.
    #
    # Also: placebo-arm numbers (e.g., "vs 2.4% with placebo") and
    # achievement thresholds ("≥5%") are filtered from the set of

    # not the claim itself.
    aggregated_span_decimals: set[str] = set()
    aggregated_span_text: list[str] = []
    valid_token_found = False
    for tok in tokens:
        ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
        if ev is None:
            failures.append(f"evidence_not_in_pool:{tok.evidence_id}")
            continue
        direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
        if tok.end > len(direct_quote):
            failures.append(
                f"span_out_of_bounds:{tok.evidence_id}:{tok.end}>{len(direct_quote)}"
            )
            continue
        if tok.start < 0 or tok.start >= tok.end:
            failures.append(
                f"span_invalid:{tok.evidence_id}:{tok.start}-{tok.end}"
            )
            continue
        # I-beatboth-001 (#1276): fetch-shell / web-boilerplate gate. A cited source whose body
        # is a CAPTCHA / security-verification interstitial, cookie-consent banner, HTTP 404/403
        # page, language-nav menu, citation-UI chrome, or social-media boilerplate can NEVER
        # ground a clinical claim — the run-#7 P0-1 leak (the 476-char Lancet CAPTCHA span
        # grounding 6 units passed because the prose verbatim-copied the junk = the self-citation
        # hole). Checked on the FULL ``direct_quote`` (a shell grounds nothing regardless of which
        # sub-span the token cites), fail-closed exactly like ``span_out_of_bounds``: the failure
        # goes non-empty and ``valid_token_found`` is NOT set, so the sentence drops and a shell
        # can never ride on a real co-token. Faithfulness-TIGHTENING only; default-on, reverts
        # byte-identical under PG_CITED_SPAN_SHELL_DETECT=0. This propagates to every render path
        # (SUPPORTS-only enrichment surfacing, verified_support_origin_count, basket_verdict)
        # through the existing verify wiring — no separate render filter (a weight_mass<=0 DROP
        # would violate §-1.3: a legit unrated/judge-off T1 source can carry weight_mass=0).
        if _cited_span_shell_detect_enabled() and _is_cited_span_shell(direct_quote):
            failures.append(f"fetch_shell_cited_span:{tok.evidence_id}")
            continue
        valid_token_found = True
        span_text = direct_quote[tok.start:tok.end]
        span_stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(span_text)
        aggregated_span_decimals |= _decimals_in(span_stripped)
        aggregated_span_text.append(span_text)

    # BUG-03 (FX-02, #1106): the empty/contentless floor runs UNCONDITIONALLY whenever a valid
    # token exists — NOT nested inside `if require_number_match and valid_token_found:` (Codex
    # iter-1 P2: nesting it left a token-only sentence verifiable when a caller passes
    # require_number_match=False). A sentence with NO content words AND no decimals AND no integers
    # (residue reduces to ".") is a token-only / punctuation-only / all-stopword "sentence" and is
    # never a valid clinical claim, regardless of number-matching. Computed on the same
    # dose/placebo/threshold-stripped text the numeric floor uses. Strictly faithfulness-TIGHTENING:
    # any content word OR number routes to the existing overlap/numeric floors (no false-drop).
    if valid_token_found:
        _bug03_stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(sentence_for_numbers)
        _bug03_stripped = _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE.sub(" ", _bug03_stripped)
        _bug03_stripped = _THRESHOLD_RE.sub(" ", _bug03_stripped)

def _strip_markers(text: str) -> str:
    """Remove bracketed ``[N]`` / ``[confidence:...]`` markers so number/word extraction reads the
    prose, not the citation index (``[1]`` must never be counted as the decimal ``1``)."""
    return _MARKER_RE.sub(" ", text or "")


def _strip_confidence_markers(text: str) -> str:
    """Remove any ``[confidence:...]`` marker(s) from ``text`` (leaving ``[N]`` citations intact) so a
    pre-labeled sentence is re-screened on its bare prose in PROMOTE mode. PURE."""
    return _CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE.sub("", text or "").strip()


def _decimals(text: str) -> "set[str]":
    """The distinct decimal number tokens in ``text`` (comma thousands-separators normalized out)."""
    return {d.replace(",", "") for d in _DECIMAL_RE.findall(text or "")}


def _content_tokens(text: str) -> "set[str]":
    """Lower-cased content tokens (>=3 chars, not a stopword)."""
    return {w for w in _WORD_RE.findall(str(text or "").lower())
            if len(w) >= 3 and w not in _GROUND_STOPWORDS}


def _span_grounds_sentence(sentence: str, span: str) -> bool:
    """Deterministic span-grounding leg of the D3 frozen-engine gate (strict_verify §9.1.3 principle).

    True iff the sentence's prose is anchored in the cited span: (a) EVERY decimal in the sentence
    appears in the span (a synthesis sentence must not invent or alter a number the cited source did
    not state), AND (b) the sentence and the span share >= ``_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP`` content words. A
    blank span never grounds. PURE. This is a CONSERVATIVE gate — it can only REFUSE to promote (drop
    to the fail-closed audit tail); it never admits a sentence the judge rejected."""
    if not span or not span.strip():
        return False
    core = _strip_markers(sentence)
    sent_nums = _decimals(core)
    if sent_nums and not sent_nums.issubset(_decimals(span)):
        return False
    return len(_content_tokens(core) & _content_tokens(span)) >= _MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP

    # unhedged categorical assertion.
    return bool(_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE.search(core))

# A confidence marker is appended ONCE per sentence; this guards against a double-append when the
# function is (defensively) called twice on already-labeled prose.
_ALREADY_LABELED_MARKER = "[confidence:"


def deviation_check_enabled() -> bool:
    """True iff the default-ON B13 deviation check is active. Requires BOTH the fine flag
    (``PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_CHECK``, default ON) AND the coarse analyst-layer flag
    (``PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS``, default ON) to be on."""
    fine_on = os.environ.get(_ENV_ENABLED, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
    coarse_on = os.environ.get(_ENV_ANALYST_KILL, "1").strip() in ("1", "true", "True")
    return fine_on and coarse_on


def _max_inflight() -> int:
    try:
        value = int(os.environ.get(_ENV_MAX_INFLIGHT, _DEFAULT_MAX_INFLIGHT) or _DEFAULT_MAX_INFLIGHT)

content.text:
I dug through the real code. The change is refutable on the hallucination lens — the safety argument depends on three deterministic floors, and when you open the files, **two of them don't do what the contract says they do, and the third can't see the novel content class at all.**

## 1. NEUTRAL *is* the ungrounded bucket. The tier's only real gate is one LLM call.

`NEUTRAL` from the judge means precisely "the span does not support this claim." The proposal converts that bucket into a keep. Its defense is "the operands are span-verified, only the relation is new." But every deterministic leg it cites (`number_not_in_any_cited_span`, `percent_not_in_cited_span`, `no_integer_overlap`, `binding_qualifier_dropped`) is **numeric**. None of them can look at a relation or a novel nominal. So for the one content class the tier newly admits, there is **zero deterministic backstop** — the entire faithfulness guarantee is the second judge call. The two floors offered as the backstop are the ones that fail below.

## 2. The hedge floor is vacuous — `_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE` is not a hedge detector.

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226` matches, among others: `remain\w*`, `requir\w*`, `represent\w*`, `reflect\w*`, `align\w*`, `poses?`, `mechanism\w*`, `framework\w*`, `logic\w*`, `broad\w*`, `typical\w*`, `dimension\w*`, `distinction\w*`, `applies`.

> "The residual mortality benefit reflects the drug's anti-inflammatory mechanism, which remains operative across all subgroups."

Three cue hits. Zero hedges. A flatly categorical mechanistic claim. Contract clause (4) ("a bare categorical assertion is ineligible") and the WHAT-STILL-GETS-KILLED line ("any unhedged interpretive assertion") are **false as implemented with this regex**. It is also sentence-level, so one hedge licenses a categorical co-clause: *"Uptake may accelerate, and the model outperforms every baseline in production."* That regex was written for a gate where numbers, percents and attributions were **categorically banned outright** (`_is_disclosable_interpretation`, :286-315) — it was never load-bearing as a standalone hedge test and cannot be reused as one.

## 3. The "named entity" floor does not exist in the code being reused.

- `_AUTHOR_ATTR_RE` (:216) is `\b[A-Z][a-z]{2,}\s+(?:et al|and\s+[A-Z][a-z]{2,})`. It matches **only** `Surname et al` / `Surname and Surname`. Not a bare surname. Not an organization, venue, trial, drug, company, statute, or country. **There is no NER anywhere in this repo.**
- In `provenance_generator.py` the author machinery is `_author_metadata_annotation` (:2586-2591), which *adds* author names **into the span the judge sees**. It is a leniency helper, not a floor.
- `_span_grounds_sentence` (:138-152) is: every decimal in span, **plus ≥2 shared content words** (`_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP`). A 40-word inference sentence clears it with two tokens in common.

So contract clause (5) — "no named entity absent from the union of cited rows' full text" — **cannot be built from the cited code**. "anti-inflammatory mechanism", "the EU AI Act", "GIP/GLP-1 agonism", "the FDA's 2023 guidance" all walk straight through.

## 4. The repo already states this judge cannot do this job.

`provenance_generator.py` ~:2378, in the comment justifying the `binding_qualifier_dropped` gate:

> *"the NLI leg is **systematically lenient to hedge dropping** and so this needs its OWN completeness gate."*

The authors built a deterministic gate **because the judge cannot adjudicate hedges.** The proposal now hands that same judge the *harder, symmetric* task — hedge-scope adjudication over decomposed premises — and makes it the sole gate. Their admitted failure mode (a) is the harmless direction. The dangerous direction is (b), and a premise-decomposition prompt is a *leading* prompt: it names "the hedged interpretive relation" as the sanctioned exception, which is exactly what an over-reaching sentence will be classified as. The repo's own note says the bias runs that way.

## 5. The keep is laundered through `is_verified`, and every reader-facing surface keys on `is_verified`, not `soft_warnings`.

Skipping the `entailment_failed` append **is** setting `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (:2930). Downstream:

- `generator/summary_table.py:1075-1088` — builds `eid -> its STRICT-VERIFY-PASSED clean sentences (is_verified only)` and renders the sentence as **that source's claim cell in the evidence table**. A cross-source inference gets printed under one paper's row, as a claim that paper never made.
- `audit_ir/citation_health.py:255` — `is_verified` sentences *are* "the rendered citation graph."
- `audit_ir/slide_deck.py:332`, `api/graph_route.py:158` — select on `is_verified`.
- `abstractive_writer.py:386,603` — counts them as verified (their own failure mode (c)).
- `claim_labeler.render_soft_flag_markers` (:100-110) maps only **registered** soft-warning ids to inline markers; `inference_tier` surfaces nothing to the reader by default.

The audit-log enumerability claim is true and irrelevant. Tier I-S ships under an **explicit interpretive disclosure**. Tier I ships in the body, in the same font, with `[#ev:a][#ev:b]` tokens attached, with **no disclosure**. That is a fabricated claim wearing two real citations — strictly worse than an uncited hallucination, because it launders through the provenance system that *is* the moat.

## The concrete path, end to end

Writer emits: `"Tirzepatide's larger reduction likely reflects dual GIP/GLP-1 agonism, a mechanism absent in the semaglutide cohort [#ev:a:10:80][#ev:b:5:60]."` Spans state only the efficacy numbers → all numeric legs **pass**. No `X et al` → author floor **silent**. Cue regex → **hits** ("likely", "reflects", "mechanism"). ≥2 content-word overlap → **passes**. Judge 1 → **NEUTRAL** (spans say nothing about receptor pharmacology). Judge 2 → "only non-span content is the hedged interpretive relation" → **INFERENCE_OK**. Ships. `is_verified=True`. Gets a summary-table row under source (a). Passes citation-health. Lands on the slide deck. **The pharmacological attribution may be simply false and no leg in the system ever looked at it.**

Finally: "byte-identical when off" is true and worthless — the claimed +0.5 to +1.0 only exists with the flag ON, and ON is where the hole is.

input.fix: If this is to be pursued at all, four things are prerequisites, and none are in the proposal: (1) A Tier-I keep MUST NOT set is_verified=True. It needs a separate admission channel (e.g. a distinct `tier` field on SentenceVerification) that every current is_verified consumer must explicitly opt into -- specifically summary_table.py:1086 (must NOT seed an evidence-table claim cell), audit_ir/citation_health.py:255, audit_ir/slide_deck.py:332, api/graph_route.py:158, abstractive_writer.py:386/603. And the rendered body must carry the same VISIBLE interpretive disclosure the analyst section (Tier I-S) already carries; audit-log-only tagging is not a reader-facing disclosure. (2) The hedge floor cannot be _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE. That bag-of-words list (remain*/requir*/represent*/mechanism*/framework*) matches unhedged categorical prose and was designed for a context where numbers and attributions were banned outright. It needs a matrix-verb / scope check that verifies the hedge actually scopes the novel relation. (3) The "named entity" floor must actually be built -- there is no NER in this repo and _AUTHOR_ATTR_RE only catches "X et al"/"X and Y". Until one exists, the only defensible deterministic substitute is: EVERY content word in the sentence outside the hedged relational connective must appear in the union of the cited spans (no novel nominal at all). That is far stricter than ">=2 content-word overlap" and would kill the mechanism-fabrication example. (4) Ship it first as a SHADOW/WARN tier: log INFERENCE_OK candidates and ship nothing, then hand-label a sample to measure the second judge's lenience bias against ground truth. The repo already documents this judge as "systematically lenient to hedge dropping" -- that bias must be quantified on real INFERENCE_OK candidates BEFORE any keep path is enabled. Absent (1)-(4), recommend rejecting C2 and taking the insight/comprehensiveness gain via C1 (the disclosed synthesis section), where the interpretive content is quarantined behind an explicit disclosure and cannot be mistaken for span-verified fact.
